title: Regular Expression Matching
problem_statement: Implement regex matching with support for "." and "*" where "." matches any character and "*" matches zero or more of the preceding element.

examples.explanation: c* can match empty, then a* matches "aa" and b matches b.

intuition: DP on positions with states handling * as zero or multiple matches.

approaches.name: DP on string and pattern indices
approaches.description: Use dp[i][j] to represent whether s[i:] matches p[j:], handling * by skipping or consuming a character.
approaches.why_not: Backtracking can be complex and error-prone for overlapping * choices.

interview_qa.q: How do you handle a * in the pattern?
interview_qa.a: Either skip the preceding element and * entirely or consume one character if the first character matches.
It cleanly handles the multiple matching choices and avoids repeated recursion.
